specs.name: compute_create_it_managed_vm
specs.description: [COMPUTE] Create a new virtual machine instance for IT-managed machines. IMPORTANT: Always ask user for ALL required fields including cost center code, login requirements, auth model, and local account. Use ID values from API responses, not names.

projectId.description: Project ID. MUST use the 'id' field from list_all_projects API response of a IT managed project.

region.description: Region code. MUST use the 'region' field from get_regions API response.

flavor.description: Flavor/instance type ID. MUST use the 'size' field from get_flavors API response, not the name.

role.description: Role ID. MUST use the 'id' field from list_all_roles API response, not the name.

secZone.description: Security zone. MUST be '3' for IT-managed machines.

costCenter.description: Cost center code. REQUIRED: Get costcenter from project details.

stabilityZone.description: Stability zone ID. MUST use the 'id' field from get_stability_zones API response.

appLayer.description: App layer ID. Ask user to choose: Web, DB, or App.

zone.description: Zone ID. Ask user to choose: Internal, BEDMZ or FEDMZ.

loginRequired.description: Login required flag. Ask user if they want login access to the machine. Use 'true' or 'false'.

authModel.description: Auth model. ALWAYS ask user: If they want login access (loginRequired=true), ask them to choose 'ldap' or 'local'. If no login access needed (loginRequired=false), enter empty string ''.

localAccount.description: Local account name. ALWAYS ask user: If they want login access (loginRequired=true), ask for the generic account username. If no login access needed (loginRequired=false), enter empty string ''.

specs.name: compute_create_self_managed_vm
specs.description: [COMPUTE] Create a new virtual machine instance for self-managed machines. IMPORTANT: Always ask user for cost center code and use ID values from API responses, not names.

projectId.description: Project ID. MUST use the 'id' field from list_all_projects API response of a self managed project.

region.description: Region code. MUST use the 'region' field from get_regions API response.

flavor.description: Flavor/instance type ID. MUST use the 'size' field from get_flavors API response, not the name.

role.description: Role ID. MUST use the 'id' field from list_all_roles API response, not the name.

secZone.description: Security zone. MUST be '0' for self-managed machines.

costCenter.description: Cost center code. REQUIRED: Get costcenter from project details.

stabilityZone.description: Stability zone ID. MUST use the 'id' field from get_stability_zones API response.

appLayer.description: App layer ID. Ask user to choose: Web, DB, or App.

image_type.description: Image type. Ask user to choose: custom (optional).

image_name.description: Image name. Ask user to provide the image name. If image_type is custom, it should start with mimg_ and it an optional field. Use the image_name field from get_images API response.

label.description: Label. Ask user to provide the label. It is an optional field.

loginRequired.description: Login required flag. Ask user if they want login access to the machine. Use 'true' or 'false'. Default is false.

specs.name: compute_extend_lease
specs.description: [COMPUTE] Extend lease for a VM (3/6/9 months)

months.description: Number of months to extend (3, 6, or 9)

specs.name: compute_get_flavors

projectId.description: Project ID. Use list_all_projects to get all projects.

role.description: Role ID (roleid) from get_roles for the desired OS. Use list_all_roles to get all roles.

region.description: Region. Use get_regions to get all regions.

instanceId.description: Instance ID and is the uuid of the server

specs.name: compute_get_ssh_key
specs.description: [COMPUTE] Get SSH key for an instance

specs.name: compute_get_stability_zones

hostnames.description: Array of hostnames to get metrics for

specs.name: compute_list_master_images

specs.name: compute_list_server_notes

specs.name: compute_power_off_instance
specs.description: [COMPUTE] Power off a server instance

specs.name: compute_power_on_instance

specs.name: compute_update_server_label
specs.description: [COMPUTE] Update the descriptive label for a server (PUT /server/{id}/label). Same payload as set_server_label.

label.description: Label text; empty string clears the label.

location.description: Location/region.This gives you the list of valid database types for the location.

environment.description: Environment (e.g., PROD, NONPROD). Use get_environments tool to get valid environments.

classification.description: Data classification (optional). This gives you the list of valid classifications for the database type and location. Use get_classifications tool to get valid classifications.

specs.name: docs_query_documentation
specs.description: [DOCS] [DOCS] Query ATSCloud documentation. This tool will always be called for any ATSCloud-related questions and provides comprehensive documentation responses with conversation context. Use this tool for any ATSCloud-related questions and provide comprehensive documentation responses with conversation context. Analyze user's question and determine if it is a ATSCloud documentation related question. If it is, call this tool to get the documentation response.

question.description: The question or query about ATSCloud documentation, features, procedures, or processes

ldap.description: User's LDAP identifier (optional, will be extracted from headers if not provided)

session_id.description: Session ID for maintaining conversation context (optional, will be auto-generated if not provided)

specs.description: [LBAAS] Attach a lbaas/loadbalancer pool to a virtual server

specs.description: [LBAAS] Create/upload a new lbaas/loadbalancer SSL certificate

vs_id.description: Virtual server ID to attach certificate to

passphrase.description: Base64 encoded passphrase for the certificate (optional)

specs.name: lbaas_create_node
specs.description: [LBAAS] Create a new lbaas/loadbalancer node.

itcnode.description: Whether this is an ITC node (default: True)

qosID.description: QoS ID (optional, will be auto-populated from project if not provided)

serviceName.description: Service name (optional, will be auto-populated from QoS if not provided)

specs.name: lbaas_create_pool
specs.description: [LBAAS] Create a new lbaas/loadbalancer pool.

specs.name: lbaas_create_poolmember
specs.description: [LBAAS] Create a new lbaas/loadbalancer pool member. First use list_nodes_for_pool to see available nodes, then create the pool member with the selected node name and desired service port.

service_port.description: Service port for the pool member (as string). Common ports: 80, 443, 8080, 8443

node.description: Server hostname/FQDN (e.g., 'or1010050054121.corp.adobe.com'). Use list_nodes_for_pool first to see available options.

pool_id.description: Pool ID to automatically attach this member to (optional but recommended)

specs.name: lbaas_create_virtualserver
specs.description: [LBAAS] Create a new lbaas/loadbalancer virtual server.

destination_port.description: Destination port for the virtual server (as string)

endpoint.description: Load balancer endpoint name (e.g., 'us-west-sj1-lab'). Use region from list_endpoints to get the endpoint value.

qosID.description: QoS ID (optional, will be auto-populated from project if not provided)

serviceName.description: Service name (optional, will be auto-populated from QoS if not provided)

specs.name: lbaas_get_node

pool_id.description: Pool ID to find compatible nodes for

specs.name: lbaas_list_nodes_for_project
specs.description: [LBAAS] List lbaas/loadbalancer nodes available for a specific project ID

specs.name: lbaas_list_pool_poolmembers
specs.description: [LBAAS] List lbaas/loadbalancer pool members attached to a specific pool

pool_id.description: Pool ID to list members for

specs.name: lbaas_list_poolmembers
specs.description: [LBAAS] List all lbaas/loadbalancer pool members for the user and their projects

specs.description: [LBAAS] List all lbaas/loadbalancer virtual servers for the user and their projects

specs.name: lbaas_set_persistence_profile
specs.description: [LBAAS] Set persistence profile on a lbaas/loadbalancer virtual server

specs.name: lbaas_set_pool_health_monitor
specs.description: [LBAAS] Set health monitor for a lbaas/loadbalancer pool

specs.name: lbaas_set_pool_lb_method
specs.description: [LBAAS] Set load balancing method for a lbaas/loadbalancer pool

specs.name: lbaas_set_virtualserver_aliases
specs.description: [LBAAS] Add or update aliases/CNAMEs for a lbaas/loadbalancer virtual server. By default new cnames are merged with existing aliases (nothing is removed). Use replace_all only when you intend to replace the entire list; empty cnames are not allowed with replace_all (MCP does not clear all aliases).Deletion is not allowed from MCP

server_id.description: Virtual server ID to set aliases for

cnames.description: CNAME strings to add (default) or the full list when replace_all is true. When replace_all is false, an empty list is a no-op and leaves existing aliases unchanged.

replace_all.description: If true, the virtual server's alias list becomes exactly cnames (minus removals). If false (default), each entry in cnames is merged with existing aliases.

specs.name: lbaas_update_node
specs.description: [LBAAS] Update an existing lbaas/loadbalancer node (description and other non-address fields)

specs.name: lbaas_update_pool
specs.description: [LBAAS] Update an existing lbaas/loadbalancer pool

specs.name: lbaas_update_virtualserver
specs.description: [LBAAS] Update an existing lbaas/loadbalancer virtual server (Note: name changes are not supported). HTTP→HTTPS redirect is controlled with http_redirect (LBaaS API field name); only valid when the VS destination port is 443.

destination_port.description: Destination port for the virtual server

http_redirect.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) HTTP→HTTPS redirect; VS must be on port 443

https_redirect.description: Deprecated alias for http_redirect; same behavior if http_redirect is omitted

specs.name: project_add_user_to_project
specs.description: [PROJECT] Add a user to a project with specified role. DO NOT check project details or list users first - just make the add user API call directly.

is_admin.description: Whether the user should have admin role (true) or view role (false)

specs.name: project_create_project
specs.description: [PROJECT] Create a new project. User will be prompted to select data classification and landscape options, then confirm the complete payload before creation. Note: Non-Public&Internal data classifications automatically require managed OS.

dataClassification.description: Data classification - MUST be one of: 'Public&Internal', 'Confidential', 'PCI', 'Restricted'

landscape.description: Environment landscape - MUST be one of: 'Dev', 'Stage', 'Prod'

iTManagedApp.description: Whether this is an IT managed application

iTManagedOS.description: Whether this uses IT managed OS (optional, default: false)

isDedicatedNetwork.description: Whether to use dedicated network (optional, default: false)

jira.description: JIRA queue key for project tracking (optional)

_confirm_create.description: Set to true to confirm project creation after reviewing details

specs.name: project_create_security_rule
specs.description: [PROJECT] Create a new security rule for a project.

_confirm_create.description: Set to true to confirm rule creation after reviewing details

specs.name: project_get_project_audit_logs
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get audit log records for a specific project.

specs.name: project_get_project_by_id
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get detailed information about a specific project by its ID.

specs.name: project_get_project_regions
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get available regions for a project.

specs.name: project_get_project_rules
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get security rules for a project.

specs.name: project_get_project_safe
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get the CyberArk safe information for a project.

specs.name: project_get_project_servers
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get all servers/VMs associated with a specific project.

specs.name: project_get_project_users
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get all users associated with a specific project.

specs.name: project_get_projects_by_email
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get all active projects for a specific user by their email address.

specs.name: project_get_recent_resources
specs.description: [PROJECT] Get recent resources created by date range and type.

resource_type.description: Type of resource (VM, Baremetal, CaaS, NFS, SMB, DaaS, Project, HPC)

specs.name: project_list_projects
specs.description: [PROJECT] List all projects for the authenticated user. Shows project details including resource counts if include_count is enabled.

include_users.description: Include member count for each project

include_count.description: Include resource counts and additional info (true/false)

specs.name: project_remove_user_from_project
specs.description: [PROJECT] Remove a user from a project. DO NOT check project details or list users first - just make the remove user API call directly.

user_id.description: User ID or email address to remove

specs.name: project_transfer_project_ownership
specs.description: [PROJECT] Transfer ownership of a project to another user. Requires confirmation.

primary_owner.description: Email of the new primary owner

secondary_owner.description: Email of the new secondary owner

_confirm_transfer.description: Set to true to confirm ownership transfer after reviewing details

specs.name: project_update_project
specs.description: [PROJECT] Update project metadata via PATCH (v2 project resource). Only project_id is always required; costCenter, wiki, team_dl, and jira are optional. secondaryOwner is optional unless deleteSecOwner is true, in which case secondaryOwner (email) is required.

secondaryOwner.description: Email of the secondary owner (optional unless deleteSecOwner is true; then required)

deleteSecOwner.description: When true, removes the secondary owner; secondaryOwner must be set to that owner's email (optional when false or omitted)

jira.description: JIRA queue key for project tracking (optional)

specs.name: project_update_project_quota
specs.description: [PROJECT] Update the instance quota for a project. Only admin users can modify project quotas.

specs.name: project_update_project_service
specs.description: [PROJECT] Update service information for a project.

payload.description: Fields to update (e.g., serviceName, serviceClassification, etc.)

specs.name: project_update_user_role
specs.description: [PROJECT] Update a user's role in a project. DO NOT check project details or list users first - just make the update role API call directly.

is_admin.description: Whether the user should have admin role (true) or view role (false)

specs.name: pm_get_cost_metrics
specs.description: [PM] ATS Cloud vs AWS-equivalent cost comparison. Returns totalATSCost, totalAWSEquivalent, totalSavings, savingsPercentage (%), plus a byService breakdown (virtualComputeITManaged, virtualComputeSelfManaged, nfs, smb, objectStorage) each with a daily cost trend. Use for ANY cost, savings, TCO, or 'how much does it cost' question. resourceType: virtualCompute = VMs only, storage = NFS/SMB/ObjectStorage only, all = everything. IMPORTANT: range is required — if not specified, ask the user to choose from: 7d, 30d, 365d, 4q, ytd, inception.

range.description: Time range (required). Ask the user if not already specified.

executive.description: LDAP to scope to that org (resolve via pm_search_user first)

costCenter.description: Cost center code (resolve via pm_search_costcenter first)

resourceType.description: Filter to VM costs, storage costs, or both (default: all)

specs.name: pm_get_dashboard_chart_data
specs.description: [PM] Returns chart/trend data for a specific metric section. Use ONLY when the user explicitly wants to visualize trends over time or a service breakdown chart. For totals and stats, use pm_get_dashboard_metrics instead (it is faster and cheaper). chartType options: resourcesByService (resources broken down by service), resourcesTrend (resources over time), usersTrend (users created over time), gatewayHits (gateway usage trends and service breakdown), actionsExecuted (portal actions over time). IMPORTANT: range is required — if not specified, ask the user.

chartType.description: Which chart data section to return.

range.description: Time range (required). Ask the user if not already specified.

executive.description: LDAP of executive to scope results

specs.name: pm_get_dashboard_metrics
specs.description: [PM] Returns summary totals and key stats for infrastructure metrics: total resources provisioned, users created, portal visits, gateway hits, actions executed. Supports executive, services, project, costCenter, resourceType filters. Use this for ANY resource count or metrics overview question. Does NOT include trend data or per-service breakdowns — use pm_get_dashboard_chart_data for those. IMPORTANT: range is required — if the user has not specified one, ask them to choose from: 7d (last 7 days), 30d (last 30 days), 365d (last year), 4q (last 4 quarters), ytd (year to date), inception (all time).

range.description: Time range (required). Ask the user if not already specified.

executive.description: LDAP of executive (resolve via pm_search_user first)

services.description: Product/project-level qosIDs (e.g. "acrobat_pro,acrobat_sign"). ALWAYS resolve via pm_get_services. Do NOT put infrastructure types like virtualCompute here.

project.description: Project ID (resolve via pm_search_project if needed)

costCenter.description: Cost center code (resolve via pm_search_costcenter if needed)

resourceType.description: Infrastructure category filter. Use for "VMs", "databases", "storage", etc.

specs.name: pm_get_executives
specs.description: [PM] Get the list of executives. Without parent param: returns top-level company executives. With parent=LDAP: returns direct reportees of that executive (name, ldap, businessUnit, reporteesCount). Use this to discover who reports to someone, then call pm_get_dashboard_metrics for each reportee to get their resource counts. Call up to 5 reportees in parallel in the same round.

parent.description: LDAP of the executive whose direct reports to list. Omit for top-level executives.

specs.name: pm_get_key_stats
specs.description: [PM] Overall ATS Cloud key statistics and KPI summary. Use ONLY when user asks for "key stats", "overall overview", or "ATS Cloud summary". Do NOT call as part of a filtered or executive-scoped query.

specs.name: pm_get_resources_by_level
specs.description: [PM] Company-wide resource breakdown by org hierarchy level. NO executive filter. Use ONLY when user asks about a specific org level. Do NOT use for executive-scoped queries — it returns company-wide data regardless. IMPORTANT: range is required — if not specified, ask the user to choose from: 7d, 30d, 365d, 4q, ytd, inception.

range.description: Time range (required). Ask the user if not already specified.

specs.name: pm_get_services
specs.description: [PM] Search available services/products by name. Returns qosID identifiers for the services param. Call when user mentions a product or service name (e.g. "Acrobat", "Photoshop"). NOTE: Do NOT use this for infrastructure types like VMs/databases/storage — use the resourceType param instead. Backend does case-insensitive substring matching, returns ALL matches.

search.description: Partial service name or qosID to search

specs.name: pm_get_user_metrics
specs.description: [PM] ATS Cloud USER distribution (headcount of resource owners) by an executive's direct reportees. Returns how many unique resource owners exist in each reportee's subtree for specified services. This is a USER/OWNER metric, NOT a resource count. Use ONLY when user asks about user distribution or owner counts. Do NOT use for 'top VPs by resources' — use pm_get_dashboard_metrics instead. IMPORTANT: range is required — if not specified, ask the user to choose from: 7d, 30d, 365d, 4q, ytd, inception.

range.description: Time range (required). Ask the user if not already specified.

executive.description: LDAP of the executive (resolve via pm_search_user)

specs.name: pm_search_costcenter
specs.description: [PM] Search cost centers by name or code (partial, case-insensitive). Use returned cost_center code in the costCenter param of pm_get_dashboard_metrics.

search.description: Partial cost center name or code

specs.name: pm_search_project
specs.description: [PM] Search projects by name (partial, case-insensitive). Use returned project_id in the project param of pm_get_dashboard_metrics.

specs.name: pm_search_user
specs.description: [PM] Search for users by name or LDAP. Use when user mentions a person by name.

specs.name: bucket_create_access_key
specs.description: [BUCKET] Create a new access key for the user

specs.name: bucket_create_bucket
specs.description: [BUCKET] Create a new object storage bucket. Requires user confirmation before creation.

name.description: Bucket name (only numbers, lowercase letters and hyphen allowed)

project_id.description: Project ID associated with the bucket

_confirm_create.description: Set to true to confirm creation after reviewing JSON

_payload.description: The JSON payload to create (required when _confirm_create is true)

specs.name: bucket_empty_bucket_trash
specs.description: [BUCKET] Empty the trash for a specific bucket.

bucket_name.description: Name of the bucket to empty trash for

specs.name: bucket_get_bucket_locations
specs.description: [BUCKET] Get available bucket locations for bucket creation. Use this when user needs to know where buckets can be created.

specs.name: bucket_list_access_keys
specs.description: [BUCKET] List all access keys for the user

specs.name: bucket_list_bucket_contents
specs.description: [BUCKET] List contents/objects inside a specific bucket.

bucket_name.description: Name of the bucket to list contents for

prefix.description: Optional prefix to filter objects (like a folder path)

specs.name: bucket_list_buckets
specs.description: [BUCKET] [LIST ONLY] Show existing buckets. Do NOT use this during bucket creation. Only use when user explicitly asks to see their bucket list.

specs.name: bucket_update_access_key_status
specs.description: [BUCKET] Update access key status (activate/deactivate/enable/disable)

key_id.description: ID of the access key to update

specs.name: bucket_update_bucket_project
specs.description: [BUCKET] Update/change the project ID for an existing bucket.

bucket_name.description: Name of the bucket to update

project_id.description: New project ID to assign to the bucket

_confirm_update.description: Set to true to confirm project update after reviewing changes

specs.name: cifs_add_pit_snapshot
specs.description: [CIFS] Add a PIT snapshot to a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_add_project_to_export
specs.description: [CIFS] Add a project to a CIFS/SMB export/share

project_id.description: Project ID to add to the export

specs.name: cifs_async_create_export
specs.description: [CIFS] Create CIFS/SMB Share asynchronously. Before creating, ask user for confirmation and help them gather optional parameters: 1) Use 'cifs_get_locations' to get available locations, 2) Ask about security requirements (security_zone, backup, expiry_date), 3) Ask about access permissions (readgroup, writegroup), 4) Ask about advanced options (purpose, landscape, serviceName, qosID, project_id). Always confirm all parameters before proceeding.

location.description: Location of the share (use 'cifs_get_locations' tool to get available locations)

storage_size.description: Storage size in GB (e.g., 2, 1). max is 500

backup.description: Backup flag - 1 to enable backup, 0 to disable. Default is 0

expiry_date.description: Expiry date like add 6,9,12 months from today (optional)

specs.name: cifs_backup_export
specs.description: [CIFS] Enable/disable backup for a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_enable_export
specs.description: [CIFS] Enable/restore a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_extend_lease
specs.description: [CIFS] Extend lease of a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_get_dashboard
specs.description: [CIFS] Get CIFS/SMB dashboard information

specs.name: cifs_get_export
specs.description: [CIFS] Get details of a specific CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_get_export_owners
specs.description: [CIFS] Get owners/users of a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_get_locations
specs.description: [CIFS] Get available CIFS/SMB locations

specs.name: cifs_list_dir_file
specs.description: [CIFS] List directory files of a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_list_exports
specs.description: [CIFS] List all CIFS/SMB exports/shares

specs.name: cifs_list_pit_snapshots
specs.description: [CIFS] Get list of PIT snapshots of a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_list_snapshots
specs.description: [CIFS] Get list of snapshots of a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_restore_dir_file
specs.description: [CIFS] Restore directory file from snapshot. Ask for confirmation before restoring the directory file and also optional parameters

specs.name: cifs_search_share_by_name
specs.description: [CIFS] Search for CIFS/SMB exports by share name only (without location requirement)

specs.name: cifs_update_acl
specs.description: [CIFS] Update ACL of a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: cifs_update_export_quota
specs.description: [CIFS] Update quota/storage for a CIFS/SMB export

specs.name: nfs_add_pit_snapshot
specs.description: [NFS] Add a PIT snapshot to an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before adding the snapshot.

specs.name: nfs_add_share_to_project
specs.description: [NFS] Add existing NFS share to a project. Ask for confirmation before adding the share to the project.

specs.name: nfs_async_create_export
specs.description: [NFS] Create NFS Share asynchronously. Before creating, ask user for confirmation and help them gather optional parameters: 1) Use 'nfs_get_locations' to get available locations, 2) Ask about security requirements (security_zone, backup, expiry_date), 3) Ask about access permissions and hosts (owner_access, group_access, root_host, read_host, write_host), 4) Ask about advanced options (purpose, landscape, serviceName, qosID, project_id). Always confirm all parameters before proceeding.

location.description: Location of the share (use 'nfs_get_locations' tool to get available locations)

owner.description: Owner of the share. Value is with @adobe.com

secondary_owner.description: Secondary owner. Get project details to get the value. Value is with @adobe.com

acl_owner.description: ACL owner. Get project details to get the value. Creator is the owner. The person who created the share is the owner. Value is without @adobe.com

mix_mode.description: Mix mode - y or n. Default is n

group.description: Group required if mix_mode is n

root_host.description: Root host (optional) in IP format (X.X.X.X). Else send empty string.

read_host.description: Read host (optional) in IP format (X.X.X.X). Else send empty string.

owner_access.description: Owner access permissions - typically 0 to 6 for directories (optional)

group_access.description: Group access permissions - typically 0 to 6 for directories (optional)

backup.description: Backup flag - 1 to enable backup, 0 to disable. Default is 0

security_zone.description: Security zone - network security classification. Value is system.

expiry_date.description: Expiry date like add 6,9,12 months from today (optional) in date format

specs.name: nfs_backup_export
specs.description: [NFS] Enable/disable backup for an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before enabling/disabling the backup.

specs.name: nfs_change_export_owner
specs.description: [NFS] Change owner of an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before changing the owner.

specs.name: nfs_enable_export
specs.description: [NFS] Enable/restore an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before enabling the export.

specs.name: nfs_extend_lease
specs.description: [NFS] Extend lease of an NFS export like add 6,9 or 12. Ask for confirmation before extending the lease.

specs.name: nfs_get_connectors
specs.description: [NFS] Get list of NFS connectors

specs.name: nfs_get_export
specs.description: [NFS] Get details of a specific NFS export.

specs.name: nfs_get_export_owners
specs.description: [NFS] Get owners/users of an NFS export

specs.name: nfs_get_export_usage
specs.description: [NFS] Get usage statistics for an NFS export

specs.name: nfs_get_host_values
specs.description: [NFS] Get host values of an NFS export (read host, root host, write host)

specs.name: nfs_get_locations
specs.description: [NFS] Get available NFS locations

specs.name: nfs_get_security_zone
specs.description: [NFS] Get security zone information for an NFS export

specs.name: nfs_list_dir_file
specs.description: [NFS] List directory files of an NFS export

specs.name: nfs_list_exports
specs.description: [NFS] List all NFS exports/shares. Value of status is E(Erro), A(Active), P(Pending), D(Deleted)

specs.name: nfs_list_pit_snapshots
specs.description: [NFS] Get list of PIT snapshots of an NFS export

specs.name: nfs_list_snapshots
specs.description: [NFS] Get list of snapshots of an NFS export

specs.name: nfs_restore_dir_file
specs.description: [NFS] Restore directory file from snapshot. Ask for confirmation before restoring the directory file and also optional parameters

specs.name: nfs_search_exports
specs.description: [NFS] Search for NFS exports

specs.name: nfs_update_export_acl
specs.description: [NFS] Update ACL permissions for an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before updating the ACL permissions.

specs.name: nfs_update_export_host
specs.description: [NFS] Update host settings for an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before updating the host settings.

write_host.description: Write host (optional) in IP format (X.X.X.X). Ask for confirmation before updating the write host.

read_host.description: Read host (optional) in IP format (X.X.X.X). Ask for confirmation before updating the read host. Send empty string if not updating.

root_host.description: Root host (optional) in IP format (X.X.X.X). Ask for confirmation before updating the root host. Send empty string if not updating.

specs.name: nfs_update_export_quota
specs.description: [NFS] Update quota/storage for an NFS export. Ask for confirmation before updating the quota.
